report_name: Identity-Related Breaches Hit 71% of Organizations, Are the Root Cause of Two-Thirds of Ransomware Attacks, Sophos Finds

threat_actors.name: Ransomware Groups
threat_actors.description: A general category representing the ransomware gangs discussed in the Sophos 'State of Identity Security 2026' report. These groups primarily use compromised identities, such as stolen employee credentials, as the initial intrusion point for their attacks.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware deployment and data theft.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Compromised identities, most often stolen employee credentials, were the initial intrusion point for 67% of all ransomware attacks studied in the Sophos report.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Implied as a primary method for stealing employee credentials, as the report recommends implementing phishing-resistant MFA and identity-focused training to counter such threats.

attack_patterns.name: Multi-Factor Authentication Request Generation
attack_patterns.description: Identified as a key threat vector, with the report recommending security awareness training focused on modern threats like MFA fatigue to mitigate credential theft.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The primary impact of ransomware attacks that originate from identity compromise, as detailed in the Sophos report.

identities.name: Energy, Oil/Gas, and Utilities Sector
identities.description: Identified in the Sophos 'State of Identity Security 2026' report as the sector with the highest rate of identity-related breaches, at 80%.

identities.name: Government Sector
identities.description: Identified in the Sophos 'State of Identity Security 2026' report as the second-highest sector for identity-related breaches, at 78%.

indicators.name: Anomalous Interactive Logon from Service Account
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Interactive Logon from Service Account
id: f1a2b3c4-d5e6-4a7b-8c9d-e0f1a2b3c4d5
status: experimental
description: Detects an interactive logon (LogonType 2) from a service account. Service accounts are typically used for non-interactive processes and should not be used for interactive logons. This activity could indicate account misuse or compromise. This is based on findings from the Sophos 'State of Identity Security 2026' report highlighting risks from non-human identities.
references:
  - https://newsbytes.ph/e-security/sophos-says-71-of-firms-hit-by-identity-related-breaches-in-past-year/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-01
tags:
  - attack.t1078
level: medium
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: security
detection:
  selection:
    EventID: 4624
    LogonType: 2
    TargetUserName|startswith:
      - 'service-account-'
      - 'svc-'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects interactive logons from accounts designated as service accounts, which could indicate misuse or compromise as highlighted by the Sophos report's focus on non-human identity risk.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous Interactive Logon from Service Account
